The plant Viola species, also known as pansy or heartsease, is a type of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). The health benefits of Viola species include their use as a natural antihistamine, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies, skin irritations, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Viola species include flavonoids, such as violine and leucanthemum, as well as anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Viola species include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, calm the nervous system, and promote overall well-being.
